foundation.title: LIST
foundation.subtitle: Dynamic Arrays
foundation.content: Python Lists are the ultimate storage tool. They can hold anything, in any order, and grow to any size.
foundation.scenario: A Digital Backpack. You can put a shirt, a map (Text), and a compass (Number) in it. You can add more pockets or remove them easily.

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Building a list of items using square brackets [].

examples.title: Appending
examples.explanation: Adding a new item to the very end of the list instantly.

examples.title: Popping
examples.explanation: Removing an item from the list by its index (like 0 for first).

foundation.keyPoint: Lists correspond to Brackets [ ]. They are MUTABLE.

foundation.title: TUPLE
foundation.subtitle: Immutable Rows
foundation.content: A Tuple is a list that CANNOT be changed once it's created. It's for data that must stay frozen.
foundation.scenario: A Permanent Passport. Your photo and name are frozen (Tuple). If you want to change them, the old passport is gone, and you must get a new one.

examples.title: Fixed Data
examples.explanation: Creating a pair of values using parentheses ( ).

examples.title: Fast Read
examples.explanation: Retrieving data from a tuple without worrying it's been edited.

examples.title: Error Logic
examples.explanation: Trying to change a tuple item causes a crash—safety by design.

foundation.keyPoint: Tuples are for Frozen data. They use Parentheses ( ).

foundation.title: DICT
foundation.subtitle: Key-Value Maps
foundation.content: A Dictionary stores data as 'Key: Value' pairs. Like a real dictionary, you use a 'Word' (Key) to find its 'Definition' (Value).
foundation.scenario: A Contacts List on your phone. To find the Number (Value), you look up the specific Name (Key). One key is tied to one value.

examples.title: The Map
examples.explanation: Building a storage map using curly braces { }.

examples.title: Lookup
examples.explanation: Retrieving a value by its name instead of its index.

examples.title: The Update
examples.explanation: Changing a value by specifying its key directly.

foundation.keyPoint: Dicts use Curly Braces { }. Access data by KEY.
